title: Citizen Science Project Finder
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive tool helping students discover and select citizen science projects based on interests, location, and available resources

learningObjectives: Select appropriate citizen science projects based on interests, location, and available resources
Explain how citizen science contributes to authentic environmental research
Evaluate the scientific rigor and accessibility of different citizen science programs
prerequisites: Basic understanding of the scientific method
Familiarity with ecology concepts such as biodiversity and climate monitoring

keyQuestions: How does citizen science contribute to real scientific research?
What makes a citizen science project scientifically valid?
How do citizen science platforms ensure data quality from volunteers?
What project best matches your interests and resources?
commonMisconceptions: Students often assume citizen science data is unreliable because volunteers are not professional scientists
Many students think citizen science is only about counting birds, not realizing the breadth of projects spanning climate, water, air, and biodiversity
assessmentOpportunities: Students create an action plan for participating in one citizen science project
Students compare two projects at different difficulty levels and evaluate their scientific contributions
Students explain how distributed data collection by volunteers enables research that professionals alone could not accomplish
